mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to price in lower future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances when unemployment crosses above 4.2%, or the signal loses predictive power when real interest rates are positive.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, validating corporate revenue growth expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term shows zero incremental predictive power over single-series models (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher short-term equity returns during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: The positive signal reverses to negative returns when the Federal Reserve initiates rate hikes, or the 5-day return correlation with industrial production growth falls below 0.05 in stable policy periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 252 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL crosses above 252.0, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting wage growth and consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive quarterly returns when either series moves in the opposite direction, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate profit expansion, which systematically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward equity returns over a 5-year rolling window, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 108 signals a contraction in manufacturing output and weakening supply-side momentum, prompting risk-off positioning and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when industrial production crosses below 108.0, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for monetary policy shifts.
